gauge.name: Mississippi River at Alton

impacts.statement: Near this height, floodwaters will reach the floor of the Lincoln Shields gazebo in West Alton, Missouri.
Near this height U.S. Highway 67 will be completely closed.
The railroad between Wood River and Alton begins flooding near this height.
At this height, the waterpark playground will become inundated.
Near this height, the southbound lanes of U.S. Highway 67 will begin flooding, forcing the detour of southbound traffic onto the northbound lanes.

Near this height, water will begin flowing over Riverlands Way between Highway 67 and Mel Price Locks and Dam.  This road is a part of the Consolidated North County levee, so this is the point when water begins gradually flowing onto the protected farmland and farm homes behind the levee.
The intersection of West Broadway (Illinois 100) and U.S. Highway 67 will begin flooding near this height.
Floodwater begins damaging property in Elsah, Illinois and in Portage des Sioux, Missouri near this height.
Near this height, floodwater should begin to impact the base of the Ardent Mills grain bins.
At this height, water will cover the sidewalk near the waterpark playground.
Near this height, the flood wall around the Argosy Casino will overtop, flooding the casino parking lot.
At this height, Bloch Marine Service at Harbor Point Yacht Club begins flooding.
Route 100 at Alton closes near this height.
Highway 100 will begin flooding at Clifton Terrace Street within 2 feet of this height.
Missouri Highway 94 from Alta Villa Road to Feltes Drive will be closed near this height.
Highway 100 begins flooding at the Clifton Terrace Street intersection near this height.
The Wood River South Levee flood gate is closed near this height.
Near this height, the outer road between Riverlands Way and the Lincoln Shields Access Road will close due to flooding.
Yachtview Drive begins flooding along the entrance to Harbor Point Yacht Club near this height.
Missouri Highway 94 between Portage Des Sioux and West Alton begins flooding.  Yachtview Drive begins flooding along the entrance to Harbor Point Yacht Club.

At this level, Highway 94 and the east end of Harbor Point Road off of Missouri Highway 94 north of West Alton begin flooding.  Also, the access road the Lincoln Shields recreation area off of U.S. Highway 367 just west of the Clark bridge begins flooding.
During open river conditions (Mel Price gates all open), Alta Villa Road off of Highway 94 begins flooding and is closed.
During open river conditions (Mel Price gates are open), Dresser Island Drive begins flooding near this level.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
